steps.title: Determine the annual multiplier
steps.content: When the value depreciates by 10% each year, 90% of the value remains. The multiplier per year is $1 - 0.10 = 0.9$.

steps.title: Calculate the 2-year depreciated value
steps.content: After 2 years, multiply the initial value by the multiplier twice: $$600 \times (0.9)^2 = 600 \times 0.81 = 486$$ This matches the correct answer - B is correct.
